sections.title: Fats
sections.narration: 3.9g of fat. omega 3 is doing a lot of the work here. fat is basically not the story here.
sections.subtitleText: 3.9g of fat. omega 3 is doing a lot of the work here. fat is basically not the story here.

sections.title: Carbs
sections.narration: 79g of carbs. fibre is doing a lot of the work here. for grains, the carb quality matters much more than the raw number.
sections.subtitleText: 79g of carbs. fibre is doing a lot of the work here. for grains, the carb quality matters much more than the raw number.

sections.title: Proteins
sections.narration: 7.1g of protein. bioavailability is one of the best parts of the protein story. protein helps, but not enough if the carb side is weak.
sections.subtitleText: 7.1g of protein. bioavailability is one of the best parts of the protein story. protein helps, but not enough if the carb side is weak.

sections.title: Minerals
sections.narration: iron at 13% daily value, magnesium at 18% daily value, zinc at 12% daily value stand out most. useful, but still pretty limited.
sections.subtitleText: iron at 13% daily value, magnesium at 18% daily value, zinc at 12% daily value stand out most. useful, but still pretty limited.

sections.title: Pros
sections.narration: the upsides first: can keep meals cheap and steady because that is a genuine category strength. works as a meal anchor because a lot of value comes from being easy to build around. usually affordable and scalable because practicality matters for staple foods.

sections.title: Cons
sections.narration: now the downsides: needs strong pairings to feel complete because the grain rarely finishes the nutrition job alone. protein quality is limited because even the better grains usually need pairing. carb load is still the main burden because that is the category risk no grain fully escapes.

closing.summary: Cornmeal does have some solid upside. but the tradeoffs keep it from climbing higher. best when you want a staple carb that actually brings something useful with it.
closing.useCaseNote: Best when you want a staple carb that actually brings something useful with it.
closing.cta: Would you rank it the same, or nah?

explanation.summary: Fats are carrying this food most, while minerals are the biggest drag. It lands in B tier.
